title: Revanchist
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Otheruses|Revan}}
{{Youmay|the organization|[[Revan/Legends|Revan]], who was also referred to as "the Revanchist" or [[Jedi Crusader|group]] of Jedi, also known as the "Jedi Crusaders"}}
{{Organization
|image=[[File:MandalorianWars-Tmeline8.jpg]]
|name=Revanchist
|type=*Resistance movement<ref name="Handbook">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]''</ref>

|founder=[[Revan/Legends|Revan]]<ref name="Handbook" />

|subgroup=
|headquarters=

|formed=[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]<ref name="Handbook" />

|fragmented=[[3960 BBY]]<ref name="KotOR CG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>
|reorganized=[[3959 BBY]], into [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire]]<ref name="KotOR CG" />
|dissolved=

|affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]<ref name="KotOR CG" />
*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]<ref name="KotOR CG" />
}}
{{Quote|Do not heed the words of the Jedi Council. The Republic will fall if we do not act now. Already the Mandalorians have taken three systems along the Rim. They will only grow more powerful with time. Come stand with me. We will use our might to help the Republic in its time of need. Join Revan and I. Together, we will battle this menace.|Malak {{Vision|Meetra Surik}}|<ref name="KotOR II game">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>}}
The '''Revanchists''', also known as the '''Jedi Crusaders''', were a renegade faction of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] who assisted the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. In [[3964 BBY]], the movement was founded by the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], who wished to take a more proactive role against the invading [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]]. Under Revan's leadership, the Revanchist [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] went against the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council's]] wishes and participated in [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] missions on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. In the [[3963 BBY|following year]], Revan uncovered the truth of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian's]] [[Battle of Cathar|genocide]] of the [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] people. Although the Jedi Council did not give the Revanchists their full support, they begrudgingly allowed the Revanchists to join the Republic Military under the banner of a [[Republic Mercy Corps]].

During the war, the Revanchists, under the leadership of Revan and his friend and fellow Jedi Knight [[Darth Malak|Malak]], led the Republic to victory in numerous battles. In [[3960 BBY]], the Jedi ultimately defeated the Mandalorians in the [[Battle of Malachor V|war's final battle]] on [[Malachor V]]. However, most of the Revanchists that were not killed during the battle fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] over the course of the [[3959 BBY|next year]]. These Jedi followed Darth Revan and Darth Malak, now the new [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] and [[Shadow Hand]], under their [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|new Sith Empire]]. The two [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] declared war on the Jedi Council and the Republic, initiating the [[Jedi Civil War]]. [[3956 BBY|Three years later]], [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Malak was defeated by Revan, who was [[Light side of the Force/Legends|redeemed]] by the Jedi Council, and Malak's Sith Empire was [[Battle of Rakata Prime|defeated]] at [[Lehon/Legends|Rakata Prime]].

==History==
===The Mandalorian Wars===
{{Quote|Several years ago, when the Mandalorian threat first arose, Revan and Malak were eager to journey to the Outer Rim to defeat the enemy of the Republic. But the council felt it best if we moved with care and caution. The true threat, the council feared, had not yet revealed itself. But Revan would not be dissuaded. Charismatic and powerful, it was inevitable many of the Order would flock to Revan's seemingly noble cause. Malak was the first to join his closest friend. Others soon followed, many of our youngest and brightest, intent on saving the galaxy from the Mandalorian threat.|Zhar Lestin|audio=ZharRevanMandalorianWars.flac|<ref name="KotOR game">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Ithorian_Revanchist.jpg|thumb|left|175px|An Ithorian Revanchist Jedi leaps into action against the Mandalorian threat.]]
In [[3964 BBY]], during the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] skirmishes between the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], a young and charismatic [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] named [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] moved throughout the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], arguing his case for [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] intervention in the war.<ref name="KotOR CG" /> At the start he was considered a lone voice among the Jedi, although he eventually gathered something of a following.<ref name="Handbook" /> The first Jedi to join his cause was his closest friend [[Darth Malak|Alek]], who aided Revan in the recruitment of Jedi to their cause.<ref name="Zhar">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''; Conversations between [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Zhar Lestin]]</ref> The Revanchists, who were also known as the Jedi Crusaders,<ref name="The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia">{{CSWECite|II|147|Jedi Crusaders}}</ref> began to gather and [[Scout/Legends|scout]] the looming [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] threat on the Outer Rim without the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council's]] support,<ref name="KotOR 9">[[Knights of the Old Republic 9|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 9]]</ref> as the Mandalorian and Republic [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]] battled ineffectually within the neutral Republic frontier.<ref name="KotOR 0">[[Knights of the Old Republic 0|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 0]]</ref>

On their way to the Outer Rim, Revan and his Jedi stopped on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] in hopes of recruiting more Jedi from the [[Jedi Tower|local Jedi temple]]. While there, Alek, Revan's longtime friend and honorary apprentice,<ref name="Handbook" /> attempted to recruit a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] named [[Zayne Carrick]]. After Alek rescued Carrick when the Padawan was freefalling in the Tarisian cityscape after a failed attempt to apprehend the [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] [[black marketeer]] [[Marn Hierogryph]], the two had a conversation about the war, in which he tried unsuccessfully to recruit the Padawan. Carrick mentioned that the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] on Taris, especially his Master [[Lucien Draay]], did not think the Jedi should be involved in the Republic's war with the Mandalorians, and that the Masters taught him and his fellow students that the Jedi were supposed to defend the Republic against the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. Alek told Carrick that sometimes one had to enter the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|darkness]] to save the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light]], and then stated that the Sith threat was dealt with decades ago.<ref name="KotOR 0" />

While Revan went to investigate events on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] and its [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]],<ref name="KotOR 9" /> most of the Revanchists, including Alek, were [[Ambush on Suurja|captured]] on the planet [[Suurja]] before its [[Fourth battle of Suurja|fourth battle]] by the Neo-Crusaders. They were taken to [[Flashpoint Stellar Research Station|Flashpoint Station]], an abandoned Republic research station that the Mandalorians had captured.<ref name="KotOR 6">[[Knights of the Old Republic 6|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 6]]</ref> The Revanchists became test subjects of a [[psychopathy|sociopath]]<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic 48">[[Knights of the Old Republic 48|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 48]]</ref> named [[Demagol]], a Mandalorian [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] who was attempting to divine the source and nature of the Jedi's [[The Force/Legends|Force]] [[Force power/Legends|abilities]], hoping to negate and/or replicate them.<ref name="KotOR 8">[[Knights of the Old Republic 8|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 8]]</ref> The Revanchists were rescued by fugitive Jedi Zayne Carrick—who had been framed for the [[Padawan Massacre]] of Taris by Carrick's Master and Revan's associate Lucien Draay—<ref name="KotOR 9" /> and Mandalorian turncoat [[Rohlan Dyre]], who had come to rescue the [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Arkanian Offshoot/Legends|Arkanian Offshoot]] [[Jarael]]. The group of Revanchists headed back to Republic space with the unconscious Demagol.<ref name="KotOR 10">[[Knights of the Old Republic 10|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 10]]</ref>

Later on the planet [[Cathar (location)/Legends|Cathar]], as they were searching for evidence of the Mandalorian's slaughter of the [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar people]], Revan, Alek, and their followers felt the deaths of thousands on Serroco as the Mandalorians [[Battle of Serroco|rained]] nuclear warheads on the surface of the planet Serroco.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic 15">[[Knights of the Old Republic 15|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 15]]</ref> A short time later, Revan, along with the Republic [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] [[Saul Karath]] and the Mandalorian leader [[Mandalore the Ultimate]], was invited to bid on the [[Exogorth/Legends|exogorth]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapons]] that the [[The Adasca BioMechanical Corporation of Arkania|Adascorp]] leader [[Arkoh Adasca]] was selling. Alek, who had been instructed by Revan to take this potential threat "out of the mix", represented the Revanchists in place of Revan,<ref name="KotOR 19">[[Knights of the Old Republic 19|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 19]]</ref> who remained on Cathar to find evidence of the Mandalorian's massacre of the Cathar people.<ref name="Insider 110">[[Star Wars Insider 110|''Star Wars Insider'' 110]]; In the "Comics" section of the magazine, John Jackson Miller says that Revan had been on Cathar since the [[Knights of the Old Republic 15|fifteenth issue]]</ref>

====Revelation on Cathar====
{{Quote|They were beaten! You didn't have to do it! One of you knew, but you didn't listen! I don't know your name—but I take up your cause. I will not remove your mask until there is justice—until the Mandalorians have been defeated once and for all. So swears…''Revan''!|Revan, after the vision of the Mandalorians slaughtering the Cathar|<ref name="KotOR 42">[[Knights of the Old Republic 42|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 42]]</ref>}}
Sometime after the [[First Battle of Omonoth|Adasca affair]], the [[Taris Siege]], and the [[Disaster on Jebble|bombardment]] of [[Jebble]], the Jedi Master Lucien Draay, a member of a secret Jedi cabal called the [[Jedi Covenant|Covenant]], was appointed to the Jedi Council. Draay claimed recent Republic intelligence reports indicated there was a blow to the Mandalorian's plans of conquest, seemingly by an insurrection in their ranks. He stated that the "interventionist" path sought by Revan was wrong both for the Jedi and the Republic. The Council approved Draay's motion which ordered the recall, or even detention, of the Revanchists.<ref name="KotOR 29">[[Knights of the Old Republic 29|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 29]]</ref> After Alek, who was going by the alias "Malak" in order to avoid the warrant for his arrest,<ref name="KotOR 31">[[Knights of the Old Republic 31|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 31]]</ref> {{C|initially as "Alek"}}<ref name="Handbook" /> testified against the Covenant on behalf of the fugitive Jedi Zayne Carrick, he was sent back to Revan and his Jedi with a final warning. He went under protest, telling the Arkanian female Jarael that he would remain "Malak" until there was an end to the war's suffering.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic 35">[[Knights of the Old Republic 35|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 35]]</ref>

[[File:RevanIsBorn-KotOR42.jpg|thumb|right|175px|On Cathar, Revan vows to defeat the Mandalorians.]]
When Malak arrived on Cathar, the Jedi Master and High Council member [[Vrook Lamar]] and a group of Jedi went to the planet to confront Revan, Malak, and the Revanchists. Lamar and the Jedi told Revan that the Mandalorians were not worth the Jedi's aid in the war, and ordered the Revanchists to disband permanently. During the discussion, Revan found and picked up a [[Revan's mask|helmet]], when some light shone on it, all the Jedi experienced a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] of the Mandalorians [[Battle of Cathar|driving]] the Cathar out to the sea. They witnessed a [[Unidentified Mandalorian female|Mandalorian female]], who had owned the helmet now in Revan's hands, pleaded with [[Cassus Fett]], Mandalore's primary strategist, to spare the Cathar, arguing that they had been beaten. However, Fett was intent on gaining revenge for what the Cathar had done to them during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], and ordered the Cathar's genocide.<ref name="KotOR 42" />

After the vision ended, Revan became enraged, shouting that the Mandalorians did not have to kill them, since they were beaten, and that while the Mandalorian female recognized this, the other Mandalorians refused to listen to her. He then donned the mask, swearing to take up the Mandalorian female's cause and not remove the mask until the Mandalorians had been defeated.<ref name="KotOR 42" /> Although the truth of what happened on Cathar was known, the Jedi still did not give Revan and Malak their full support. However, Revan managed to organize the Revanchists under the banner of a Republic mercy corps. The Jedi Council allowed Revan, Malak, and the Revanchists to participate in the Mandalorian Wars.<ref name="KotOR 49">[[Knights of the Old Republic 49|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 49]]</ref> Both Malak and Revan were promoted to the rank of [[General/Legends|general]].<ref name="KotOR 42" /> The revelation of what occurred on Cathar also convinced many more Jedi to join the Revanchists.<ref name="KotOR CG" />

====The final battles====
{{Quote|Revan and Malak were heroes, the great saviors of the Republic.|Dorak|audio=DorakRevanandMalak.flac|<ref name="KotOR game" />}}
The Revanchists quickly became vital to the Republic's war effort. Revan and Malak proved to be capable commanders in the field and swiftly rose through the ranks, with Revan's tactics and Malak's righteous fury unmatched.<ref name="The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia 2">{{CSWECite|II|279|Malak, Darth}}</ref> Revan, who had been given full operational control of the Republic's forces,<ref name="KotOR CG" /> used his newly found authority to spearhead the Republic war effort and under his leadership, the all–but–defeated Republic Navy began to push the Mandalorians back.<ref name="KotOR game" /> However, at some point during the Mandalorian Wars, Revan and Malak found a [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Star Map]] in the ancient [[Rakatan ruins (Dantooine)|burial mounds]] near the Dantooine Jedi Enclave. After successfully passing the "trials" set for them by the [[Rakatan guardian droid|guard droid]] that had been left behind by the so called "[[Infinite Empire/Legends|builders]]," they were given access to the Star Map. Moments before they went beyond the door that led to the dark side artifact, however, Malak warned Revan that stepping beyond this threshold would mean that they could never return to the Jedi Order. Whether or not Revan considered this, or even cared, as he paced in front of the sealed door is unknown; but if he had any doubts, he swiftly cast them aside.<ref name="KotOR game" /> At some point during the latter part of the Mandalorian Wars, during the [[Kanz Disorders/Legends|Kanz Disorders]], the Revanchists raided an isolated slave colony on [[Lorrd II]], the second moon of [[Lorrd/Legends|Lorrd]]. They freed the slave population on the moon. However, this was not done out of a sense of justice, rather, this was to acquire [[Lorrdian gemstone/Legends|Lorrdian gemstones]] for use in [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] construction, because the gemstones were imprinted with the Force. It was said that the Lorrdian gemstones allowed the wielder to anticipate a sentient being's actions.<ref name="Death in the Slave Pits of Lorrd">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/fiction/feature20090531/index.html?page=1|text=Death in the Slave Pits of Lorrd: A Hyperspace Exclusive|int=Death in the Slave Pits of Lorrd}}</ref>

Later during the wars, in the year [[3961 BBY]], Revan found another Star Map on the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] homeworld of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. Sometime after he found the Kashyyyk Star Map, Revan discovered the ancient Sith world of [[Malachor V]], which was considered taboo to the Mandalorians.<ref name="KotOR CG" /> On its surface, Revan was quickly assaulted by the destructive dark side energies that permeated the world, yet through the strength of his will, Revan was able to withstand the dark power without succumbing to it. Soon thereafter, Revan stumbled upon the [[Trayus Academy]], an abandoned training center built by the ancient [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]]. While unearthing the planet's buried secrets, Revan learned the location and significance of the planet [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], the homeworld of the ancient [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith species]].<ref name="KotOR II game" />

[[File:Revan_Malak_Sith_Lords_EGTW.png|thumb|150px|The fall of Revan and Malak|left]]
In the year [[3960 BBY]], Revan forced a [[Battle of Malachor V|final battle]] with the Mandalorians over Malachor V. Revan dueled Mandalore the Ultimate on the planet's surface, but learned from his defeated enemy that the Mandalore had been [[Mind trick/Legends|influenced]] by a group of Sith to attack the Republic. After he defeated Mandalore, he forced the surviving Mandalorians to surrender, ordering his forces to dismantle their weapons and [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droids]]. Revan also denied the Mandalorians their fallen leader's [[Mandalore's Mask|ancestral mask]], denying the vanquished force the right to choose a new leader. Revan condemned the Mandalorians to perpetual exile, causing many to become [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]], and [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]].<ref name="KotOR game" />

Revan and Malak followed Mandalore's instructions to the planet [[Rekkiad]], where they discovered the [[Dramath the Second's tomb|tomb]] of the Sith Lord [[Dramath the Second]]. From the Sith's [[Dramath the Second's datacron]], the pair learned of the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|"true" Sith Empire]], and left Mandalore's mask in the [[Dramath the Second's sarcophagus|sarcophagus]] while they departed for this new enemy. However, Revan and Malak were broken by the [[Tenebrae|Sith Emperor]], who sent the pair back to the Republic to prepare for his invasion. Revan and Malak would break free of their conditioning, but they were still corrupted—they established their own [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]] and rediscovered the ancient [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] known as the [[Star Forge]]. With the exception of [[Meetra Surik]], the Human female who would later be known as the Jedi Exile, most of the Revanchist Jedi who survived the battle became [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], serving the empire of their former leaders. Revan and Malak, who had each taken the "[[Darth/Legends|Darth]]" title,<ref name="T8: TJCW">{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 8: The Jedi Civil War|int=Timeline 8: The Jedi Civil War}}</ref> declared war on the Jedi Council<ref name="Heritage of the Sith">{{InsiderCite|88|Heritage of the Sith}}</ref> and the Galactic Republic that they once sought to defend.<ref name="KotOR CG" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|Despite the Mandalorians' brutal aggression, the Council decreed that no Jedi should take part in the fight. As the war grew worse, however, a splinter group of rebels formed within the order, determined to rally to the Republic's defense. The splinter group was led by Revan and Malak. Joining Onasi and the rest of the Republic's troops, Revan and Malak turned the war around.|Jedi Master Gnost Dural reflects on the Revanchists' involvement in the Mandalorian Wars|<ref name="T9: TMW" />}}
Shortly after Revan [[Light side of the Force/Legends|redeemed]] himself by defeating Malak and his Sith Empire, he left for the Unknown Regions to search for the reconstituted remnants of the Sith Empire, which both he and Malak encountered after the Mandalorian Wars. Approximately [[3681 BBY|three centuries]] after Malak's death,<ref name="Timeline 1: Treaty of Coruscant">{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 1: Treaty of Coruscant|int=Timeline 1: Treaty of Coruscant}}</ref> the True Sith Empire that Revan went to battle<ref name="T8: TJCW" /> launched an attack on the Republic. This ignited a [[Great Galactic War|war]] that lasted for decades until the [[Tenebrae|Sith Emperor]] signed a [[Treaty of Coruscant|treaty]] with the Republic.<ref name="Star Wars: The Old Republic">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''</ref> Images of members of the Revanchist organization were featured in two [[Holodocumentary|holodocumentaries]] produced by the [[Kel Dor/Legends|Kel Dor]] [[Jedi archivist/Legends|Jedi archivist]] [[Gnost-Dural]] for the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Jedi Archives]].<ref name="T8: TJCW" /><ref name="T9: TMW" />

==Members==
The Jedi who joined Revan experienced regular interaction with the Republic military, sharing the mess halls, battleships, and front lines. While the Revanchists were hailed as heroes by the Republic and its citizens, some of those Jedi abandoned the war when they saw how thoughtlessly Revan approached the dark side. Others were spies that were commissioned by the Jedi Council to report on Revan and his Jedi's actions.<ref name="KotOR CG" /> When Revan set off to challenge the invading Mandalorians, Revan's closest friend Malak was the first to join his cause.<ref name="Zhar" /><ref name="Handbook" /> The Jedi Master [[Arren Kae]], who had trained Revan, joined her former Padawan in his war effort against the Mandalorians.<ref name="Convo with Kreia">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', Conversation with Kreia about the [[Echani/Legends|Echani]] [[Handmaiden/Legends|Handmaiden]] [[Brianna]]'s mother, Arren Kae</ref> The Jedi [[Cariaga Sin]], [[Nisotsa]], [[Talvon Esan]], and [[Xaset Terep]] were all recruited by Malak, along with Meetra Surik, the Human female who would eventually come to be known as the Jedi Exile.<ref name="Exile's vision">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''; Vision to the Jedi Exile</ref> Other Jedi who were a part of the Revanchist organization included [[Cale Berkona]],<ref name="KotOR 8" /> [[Ferroh]],<ref name="KotOR 42" /> [[Duqua Dar]], [[Acaadi]],<ref name="ID">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/iridonian|text=Iridonian Darkness|int=Iridonian Darkness}}</ref> [[Darth Voren|Voren Renstaal]],<ref name="TBODR">"[[The Betrayal of Darth Revan]]"</ref> an Ithorian Jedi,<ref name="Image">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''; [[:File:Ithorian Revanchist.jpg|Image]] of the Ithorian Revanchist</ref> a [[Unidentified Human female Revanchist|Human female Jedi]], and the Jedi Master [[Zez-Kai Ell]]'s [[Zez-Kai Ell's Padawan|Padawan]].<ref name="KotOR II game" />

==Apparel==
The Revanchists typically garbed themselves in traditional Jedi robes, which they supplemented with armor plating, similar to that worn by the Republic's soldiers. [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|Double-bladed lightsabers]] were in great vogue amongst the Revanchists, with many of the renegade Jedi taking advantage of the increased kill power afforded to them by the weapon's twin blades.

==Behind the scenes==
The Revanchists were first mentioned in [[BioWare]]'s [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', as part of the backstory for the events in the game.<ref name="KotOR game" /> The organization were featured prominently in the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'' comic series]], written by [[John Jackson Miller]] and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]]. The group was first identified as such in "[[The Taris Holofeed: Siege Edition]]," which was featured in the comic series' [[Knights of the Old Republic 15|fifteenth issue]]. Further details on the Revanchists were featured in [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] and the ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''. The organization received two separate entries in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', under the names "Revanchists" and "Jedi Crusaders." Images of Jedi affiliated with the Revanchist organization were featured in the eighth and ninth entries of the ''[[Galactic Timeline]]'', an online video series created to elaborate upon the background for the upcoming PC video game ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''. The entries, titled [[Timeline 8: The Jedi Civil War|''The Jedi Civil War'']] and [[Timeline 9: The Mandalorian Wars|''The Mandalorian Wars'']], respectively, are available through [[The Holonet]] section of ''The Old Republic''{{'s}} [http://www.swtor.com official website].<ref name="T8: TJCW" /><ref name="T9: TMW" />
